---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: e7c31e234a44e47d84bc1238979ceef6428657aa
findings:
  - { id: judged-ac3-disclosure-token-unrelated-match, kind: judged, text: "AC-3's disclosure check (hasRitualDisclosure, instructionritual_test.go:67-93) is satisfied by the bare substrings \"retired\" or \"grandfathered\" appearing ANYWHERE in the file, in any context. The build already discovered this exact vacuous-satisfaction class during its own bring-up — the bare word \"superseded\" in unrelated sticky-disposition prose would have silently cleared the motivating defect, so it was tightened to \"is superseded\" (R4-I-72) — but \"retired\" and \"grandfathered\" were left as bare words. A future skill that teaches `verdi board commit` as the current step while, in an unrelated paragraph, saying e.g. \"the waivers verb is retired\" passes AC-3's tripwire with no disclosure of the ritual itself. The spec's clearing condition is a file that \"names the command and discloses its retired or grandfathered status in the same breath\"; the implementation requires no association between the disclosure token and the ritual at all. The spec's disclosed lexical-tripwire caveat covers paraphrase evasion of the phrase set, not disclosure-by-unrelated-text, so this sits outside the disclosed limit. (confidence 0.60)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Same substantive finding as the prior align run (commit 6ac5c92, id judged-ac3-disclosure-bare-substrings), re-surfaced under fresh judge phrasing. DC-3 deliberately defines the AC-3 disclosure check as bare-substring presence anywhere in the same file, not proximity-anchored, so the rewrite-to-disclose alternative stays satisfiable. R4-I-72 (ledger) already examined this exact false-clearing risk for retired and grandfathered -- the same class narrowed for superseded to is-superseded -- and left them bare after confirming neither collides in either real enumerated file. CO-2 discloses the whole tripwire as an imperfect lexical heuristic, hardening deferred to a future judged-sweep-shaped story. No new technical claim versus the prior run. Accepted as a disclosed, previously-examined residual, not a new defect." }
  - { id: judged-ac2-real-tree-protection-is-vacuous, kind: judged, text: "AC-5's green run proves less than its spec framing implies: after DC-4's retirement deleted .claude/skills entirely (commit f02a06b), the only enumerated real instruction file is the repo-root CLAUDE.md, which names CLI verbs as bare backticked words (\"`gate`\", \"`board`\", \"`close`\", ...) with no \"verdi \" prefix — a shape DC-1 deliberately excludes from extraction. So AC-2's verb validation finds zero references on the real tree (zero-found, not checked-and-clean), and the sibling drift class AC-2 exists to catch — a verb removed outright while CLAUDE.md still lists it — would produce zero findings today. The vacuity is honestly disclosed in a test comment (instructionverbs_test.go:10-16), but only there: AC-5's zero-findings pass and the green make verify present the tree as verified-conformant, while the one real file most likely to go stale is invisible to the check. Three-valued honesty would want this surfaced as disclosed-as-unproven at the gate/AC level, not in a comment. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Same substantive finding as the prior align run (commit 6ac5c92, id judged-ac2-vacuous-on-real-tree), re-surfaced under fresh non-deterministic judge phrasing on a fresh non-frozen run (X-14: report regeneration does not preserve prior dispositions by finding id). DC-1 explicitly discloses this exact gap: AC-2 extraction targets only the verdi-plus-verb invocation shape inside backtick spans, never a bare backticked verb word with no prefix; CLAUDE.md names CLI verbs as bare backticked words, so it passes AC-2 vacuously today, disclosed by DC-1, not hidden, with the reversible remedy named: widen the pattern later if a real drift case demonstrates the gap matters in practice. No new technical claim versus the prior run. Accepted as designed, not a defect." }
  - { id: judged-ac2-placeholder-token-false-positive, kind: judged, text: "extractVerdiVerbRefs' token grammar (verdiVerbInvocationRe, instructionverbs_test.go:85) captures the next non-whitespace run after \"verdi\", so legitimate documentation that quotes usage syntax in a code span — e.g. `verdi \u003cverb\u003e --help`, or a fenced block reproducing dispatch.go's own \"usage: verdi \u003cverb\u003e\" banner — extracts \"\u003cverb\u003e\" as a candidate, execs it, receives the unknown-verb banner, and fails the file. AC-4's clean fixture claims to prove \"the checks do not also false-positive on legitimate content\", but it only exercises the AC-3 disclosure path plus real bare verbs; no fixture covers placeholder/usage-syntax mentions, which are a normal shape for instruction files documenting a CLI. Currently latent (neither real instruction file quotes usage syntax), it converts to a false red on the self-hosted AC-5 gate the first time CLAUDE.md or a future SKILL.md documents the general invocation form. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Same substantive finding as the prior align run (commit 6ac5c92, id judged-ac2-non-verb-token-false-positive), re-surfaced under fresh judge phrasing; the underlying code (internal/specalign) is unchanged by this merge (verdi.bindings.yaml plus unrelated residue/audit files only). Previously verified live against the real binary: verdi --help, verdi \u003cverb\u003e, and verdi -v each print the identical top-level unknown-verb banner, confirming the extractor would false-positive on a flag or usage-placeholder token, but confirmed latent, not live -- neither real enumerated file contains one today and go test ./internal/specalign/... is green, so AC-5 holds on the real tree. A disclosed extraction-precision residual in the same forward-looking spirit as DC-1/CO-2, not a violation of any AC as currently authored." }
digest: sha256:671f55f55149bf0ad5e389b4ecc238f98dba6768c19b092c9fd59a428e591d07
integrity: sha256:7507ce5722c10f3356c652ea6b289d6e1a9191fbcb12ed5270f3296c58801c17
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ac3-disclosure-token-unrelated-match\",\"text\":\"AC-3's disclosure check (hasRitualDisclosure, instructionritual_test.go:67-93) is satisfied by the bare substrings \\\"retired\\\" or \\\"grandfathered\\\" appearing ANYWHERE in the file, in any context. The build already discovered this exact vacuous-satisfaction class during its own bring-up — the bare word \\\"superseded\\\" in unrelated sticky-disposition prose would have silently cleared the motivating defect, so it was tightened to \\\"is superseded\\\" (R4-I-72) — but \\\"retired\\\" and \\\"grandfathered\\\" were left as bare words. A future skill that teaches `verdi board commit` as the current step while, in an unrelated paragraph, saying e.g. \\\"the waivers verb is retired\\\" passes AC-3's tripwire with no disclosure of the ritual itself. The spec's clearing condition is a file that \\\"names the command and discloses its retired or grandfathered status in the same breath\\\"; the implementation requires no association between the disclosure token and the ritual at all. The spec's disclosed lexical-tripwire caveat covers paraphrase evasion of the phrase set, not disclosure-by-unrelated-text, so this sits outside the disclosed limit.\",\"confidence\":0.6},{\"id\":\"ac2-real-tree-protection-is-vacuous\",\"text\":\"AC-5's green run proves less than its spec framing implies: after DC-4's retirement deleted .claude/skills entirely (commit f02a06b), the only enumerated real instruction file is the repo-root CLAUDE.md, which names CLI verbs as bare backticked words (\\\"`gate`\\\", \\\"`board`\\\", \\\"`close`\\\", ...) with no \\\"verdi \\\" prefix — a shape DC-1 deliberately excludes from extraction. So AC-2's verb validation finds zero references on the real tree (zero-found, not checked-and-clean), and the sibling drift class AC-2 exists to catch — a verb removed outright while CLAUDE.md still lists it — would produce zero findings today. The vacuity is honestly disclosed in a test comment (instructionverbs_test.go:10-16), but only there: AC-5's zero-findings pass and the green make verify present the tree as verified-conformant, while the one real file most likely to go stale is invisible to the check. Three-valued honesty would want this surfaced as disclosed-as-unproven at the gate/AC level, not in a comment.\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"ac2-placeholder-token-false-positive\",\"text\":\"extractVerdiVerbRefs' token grammar (verdiVerbInvocationRe, instructionverbs_test.go:85) captures the next non-whitespace run after \\\"verdi\\\", so legitimate documentation that quotes usage syntax in a code span — e.g. `verdi \u003cverb\u003e --help`, or a fenced block reproducing dispatch.go's own \\\"usage: verdi \u003cverb\u003e\\\" banner — extracts \\\"\u003cverb\u003e\\\" as a candidate, execs it, receives the unknown-verb banner, and fails the file. AC-4's clean fixture claims to prove \\\"the checks do not also false-positive on legitimate content\\\", but it only exercises the AC-3 disclosure path plus real bare verbs; no fixture covers placeholder/usage-syntax mentions, which are a normal shape for instruction files documenting a CLI. Currently latent (neither real instruction file quotes usage syntax), it converts to a false red on the self-hosted AC-5 gate the first time CLAUDE.md or a future SKILL.md documents the general invocation form.\",\"confidence\":0.5}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-20, commit: e7c31e234a44e47d84bc1238979ceef6428657aa }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/instruction-conformance@e7c31e234a44e47d84bc1238979ceef6428657aa, spec/instruction-conformance@38fd824509298f49fd5f393cd083d3841ab69fe0], digest: sha256:671f55f55149bf0ad5e389b4ecc238f98dba6768c19b092c9fd59a428e591d07, integrity: sha256:7507ce5722c10f3356c652ea6b289d6e1a9191fbcb12ed5270f3296c58801c17, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-ac3-disclosure-token-unrelated-match** [accepted-deviation]: AC-3's disclosure check (hasRitualDisclosure, instructionritual_test.go:67-93) is satisfied by the bare substrings "retired" or "grandfathered" appearing ANYWHERE in the file, in any context. The build already discovered this exact vacuous-satisfaction class during its own bring-up — the bare word "superseded" in unrelated sticky-disposition prose would have silently cleared the motivating defect, so it was tightened to "is superseded" (R4-I-72) — but "retired" and "grandfathered" were left as bare words. A future skill that teaches `verdi board commit` as the current step while, in an unrelated paragraph, saying e.g. "the waivers verb is retired" passes AC-3's tripwire with no disclosure of the ritual itself. The spec's clearing condition is a file that "names the command and discloses its retired or grandfathered status in the same breath"; the implementation requires no association between the disclosure token and the ritual at all. The spec's disclosed lexical-tripwire caveat covers paraphrase evasion of the phrase set, not disclosure-by-unrelated-text, so this sits outside the disclosed limit. (confidence 0.60) — Same substantive finding as the prior align run (commit 6ac5c92, id judged-ac3-disclosure-bare-substrings), re-surfaced under fresh judge phrasing. DC-3 deliberately defines the AC-3 disclosure check as bare-substring presence anywhere in the same file, not proximity-anchored, so the rewrite-to-disclose alternative stays satisfiable. R4-I-72 (ledger) already examined this exact false-clearing risk for retired and grandfathered -- the same class narrowed for superseded to is-superseded -- and left them bare after confirming neither collides in either real enumerated file. CO-2 discloses the whole tripwire as an imperfect lexical heuristic, hardening deferred to a future judged-sweep-shaped story. No new technical claim versus the prior run. Accepted as a disclosed, previously-examined residual, not a new defect.
- **judged-ac2-real-tree-protection-is-vacuous** [accepted-deviation]: AC-5's green run proves less than its spec framing implies: after DC-4's retirement deleted .claude/skills entirely (commit f02a06b), the only enumerated real instruction file is the repo-root CLAUDE.md, which names CLI verbs as bare backticked words ("`gate`", "`board`", "`close`", ...) with no "verdi " prefix — a shape DC-1 deliberately excludes from extraction. So AC-2's verb validation finds zero references on the real tree (zero-found, not checked-and-clean), and the sibling drift class AC-2 exists to catch — a verb removed outright while CLAUDE.md still lists it — would produce zero findings today. The vacuity is honestly disclosed in a test comment (instructionverbs_test.go:10-16), but only there: AC-5's zero-findings pass and the green make verify present the tree as verified-conformant, while the one real file most likely to go stale is invisible to the check. Three-valued honesty would want this surfaced as disclosed-as-unproven at the gate/AC level, not in a comment. (confidence 0.50) — Same substantive finding as the prior align run (commit 6ac5c92, id judged-ac2-vacuous-on-real-tree), re-surfaced under fresh non-deterministic judge phrasing on a fresh non-frozen run (X-14: report regeneration does not preserve prior dispositions by finding id). DC-1 explicitly discloses this exact gap: AC-2 extraction targets only the verdi-plus-verb invocation shape inside backtick spans, never a bare backticked verb word with no prefix; CLAUDE.md names CLI verbs as bare backticked words, so it passes AC-2 vacuously today, disclosed by DC-1, not hidden, with the reversible remedy named: widen the pattern later if a real drift case demonstrates the gap matters in practice. No new technical claim versus the prior run. Accepted as designed, not a defect.
- **judged-ac2-placeholder-token-false-positive** [accepted-deviation]: extractVerdiVerbRefs' token grammar (verdiVerbInvocationRe, instructionverbs_test.go:85) captures the next non-whitespace run after "verdi", so legitimate documentation that quotes usage syntax in a code span — e.g. `verdi <verb> --help`, or a fenced block reproducing dispatch.go's own "usage: verdi <verb>" banner — extracts "<verb>" as a candidate, execs it, receives the unknown-verb banner, and fails the file. AC-4's clean fixture claims to prove "the checks do not also false-positive on legitimate content", but it only exercises the AC-3 disclosure path plus real bare verbs; no fixture covers placeholder/usage-syntax mentions, which are a normal shape for instruction files documenting a CLI. Currently latent (neither real instruction file quotes usage syntax), it converts to a false red on the self-hosted AC-5 gate the first time CLAUDE.md or a future SKILL.md documents the general invocation form. (confidence 0.50) — Same substantive finding as the prior align run (commit 6ac5c92, id judged-ac2-non-verb-token-false-positive), re-surfaced under fresh judge phrasing; the underlying code (internal/specalign) is unchanged by this merge (verdi.bindings.yaml plus unrelated residue/audit files only). Previously verified live against the real binary: verdi --help, verdi <verb>, and verdi -v each print the identical top-level unknown-verb banner, confirming the extractor would false-positive on a flag or usage-placeholder token, but confirmed latent, not live -- neither real enumerated file contains one today and go test ./internal/specalign/... is green, so AC-5 holds on the real tree. A disclosed extraction-precision residual in the same forward-looking spirit as DC-1/CO-2, not a violation of any AC as currently authored.
